title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 aims to regulate and utilize artificial intelligence in government programs related to the Small Business Administration. Its purpose is unclear without further details or description.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a policy framework likely related to the regulation of artificial intelligence in the agricultural industry, aiming to provide a structured approach to its development and use. The exact scope of the policy remains unclear, but it may address issues such as AI adoption, data security, and farmer benefits.

title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act aims to establish a framework for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence in the United States. This act may promote uniformity in AI policies across the country.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This bill aims to prevent artificial intelligence systems from charging excessively high prices and unfairly controlling wages, promoting a more equitable and transparent AI market. It is intended to be enacted in 2025, though details of the bill remain unknown.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a proposed law aimed at preventing the theft of American artificial intelligence models. If passed, it would likely establish penalties and regulations to protect US intellectual property and technology.

title: Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026 likely sets out policies and regulations for the research and use of artificial intelligence within the court system. It may aim to ensure the effective and responsible integration of AI technology in court proceedings.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: This policy document aims to protect consumers from deceptive AI by establishing regulations and guidelines. The "Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act" likely seeks to safeguard individuals from misleading AI practices and ensure transparency in AI interactions.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a legislation aimed at managing risks associated with artificial intelligence. It likely establishes guidelines and regulations for the development, deployment, and use of AI technology to ensure safety and security.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote the development and use of Artificial Intelligence in the financial services industry. The act's purpose is to create an environment that encourages innovation while ensuring the safety and security of financial transactions.

title: HBCU AI Research Leadership Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The HBCU AI Research Leadership Act aims to support Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in advancing Artificial Intelligence research and development. This act likely aims to provide resources and opportunities for HBCUs to increase their presence and expertise in the AI field.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at introducing students to AI concepts from an early age. The act seeks to promote AI literacy among K-12 students, preparing them for a future where artificial intelligence plays a significant role.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers by preventing false or misleading information presented by artificial intelligence systems, likely to ensure trust and transparency in AI-driven transactions. This act may establish regulations and enforcement mechanisms to hold AI developers accountable for deceptive AI usage.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy that likely deals with the regulation of artificial intelligence models, focusing on the responsible development and deployment of AI technology.
